findings: Found 2 new medium tail-drift issues: active docs still teach project-local .fabric/knowledge as source of truth, and --fix-knowledge still advertises demote/archive git-mv repairs that store cutover currently disables.

issues_found.title: User and schema docs still teach project-local .fabric/knowledge as the knowledge source after store-only cutover
issues_found.description: docs/USER-QUICKSTART.md:10 says team knowledge is stored under .fabric/knowledge, :20 tells developers to author .fabric/knowledge/<type>, and :60 shows pending there; docs/data-schema.md:15-16 and :26 define the same repo/home roots, while AGENTS.md:5 and :19 now require mounted stores only.
issues_found.fix_direction: Rewrite onboarding and data-schema storage sections around mounted stores/current write store paths, remove direct authoring of project-local .fabric/knowledge, and mark any old dual-root examples as legacy migration material.

issues_found.title: doctor --fix-knowledge advertises demote/archive git-mv repairs that store cutover currently no-ops
issues_found.description: packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts:93-97 and :1577 still advertise demote/archive/frontmatter+git mv fixes and docs/configuration.md:289-291 says --fix-knowledge rewrites maturity and relocates stale entries, but packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts:1834-1838 says store-aware versions must be implemented before re-enabling and :657-670 returns empty candidates for those inspections.
issues_found.fix_direction: Either re-enable store-aware demote/stale/pending archive inspections over ~/.fabric/stores/*/knowledge, or narrow CLI help/docs/previews to the repair actions that actually still run after the store cutover.
